rulings.Case Title: DIAMOND, ALLAN B., AS CHAPTER 11 TRUSTEE OF HOWREY VS. ENERGY INCORPORATED NV, LLC, A NEVADA LIMITED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 3, PETITIONER DIAMOND, ALLAN B., AS CHAPTER 11 TRUSTEE OF HOWREY LLP'S Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Confirm Contractual Arbitration Award Granted. No opposition filed. The Court confirms the arbitration award in the amount of $824,005.80 plus arbitration fees and expenses in the amount of $13,900, for a total award of $837,905.80, plus interest thereon at the rate of 10% per annum from the date of entry of judgment. The Court will sign the proposed Judgment submitted with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT VS. ROE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complt
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 7, PLAINTIFF DEIDRENNE ASHCRAFT'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO File First Amended Complaint The Court discloses and will disclose at the hearing that plaintiff's counsel Charles Geerhart is a pro tem judge handling discovery matters. This does not affect Judge Miller's ability to be fair and impartial. Denied, without prejudice. Plaintiff's motion and the declaration of Mr. Paoli do not comply with CRC 3.1324(b). Mr. Paoli does not address: "(1) The effect of the amendment; (2) Why the amendment is necessary and proper; (3) When the facts giving rise to the amended allegations were discovered; and (4) The reasons why the request for amendment was not made earlier." (CRC 3.1324(b).) The evidence proffered in reply is improper and will not be considered. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: KARMA QUICK VS. JONATHAN FRIED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion For Protective Order Re Cart Services
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 2: Plaintiff KARMA QUICK'S Motion For Protective Order Re Cart Services Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part. Plaintiff's deposition shall proceed with Defendant providing CART services for Plaintiff. Such services shall not interfere with the regular court reporter. Plaintiff is responsible for paying for CART services for conversations with her own counsel and other off the record conversations. No sanctions are awarded to or against either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MARIA DE LA LUZ BELMONTE VS. BAY AREA PROPERTIES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Interrogatory Answers, Responses To Req. For Production Of Documents And Statement Of Damages
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 3: Defendant BALWANTSINGH THAKORIS DBA WARFIELD HOTEL'S Motion To Compel Interrogatory Answers, Responses To Request For Production Of Documents And Statement Of Damages Pro Tem Judge Elizabeth Riles, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted. Plaintiff is to provide responses to Demands for Inspection and Production of Documents, Form Interrogatories and Special Interrogatories without objections. Plaintiff shall provide a response to the Request for Statement of Damages. The Plaintiff's responses to all of these requests are due within 10 days of the date of this order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to eriles@brhrlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ANDRE PATTERSON VS. ANDREA R. MILANO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss;
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 10, DEFENDANTS CATHOLIC CHARITIES, MARINELLA GONCALVES Notice Of Motion And Motion To Dismiss; Denied. Dismissal is not warranted. Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves move to dismiss the Complaint based on a Court order, filed on March 11, 2013, where the Court granted Defendants Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint per CCP § 425.18. However, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves were not the moving party on the motion to strike SLAPPback Complaint. The dismissal of the Complaint against Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves based on the Court Order granting Milano and Littler Mendelson, PC's motion is not warranted. To the extent that Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves argue the merits of their own anti-SLAPPback motion in the instant motion to dismiss, Defendants Catholic Charities and Goncalves did not state these grounds for relief in their Notice of Motion. The court cannot grant different relief, or relief on different grounds, than stated in the notice of motion. (Luri v. Greenwald (2003) 107 CA4th 1119, 1124, 132 CR2d 680, 684.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. Moving party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the moving party's counsel in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: SHAWN REFOUA VS. BARWORKS, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Tuesday, August 20, 2013, Line 12, DEFENDANT BARWORKS, INC.'S MOTION FOR WITHDRAWAL OF ATTORNEY OF RECORD Hearing required. No opposition filed. The Court is inclined to grant the motion. Counsel must submit a proposed order on the mandatory Judicial Council form clarifying in item 1 the names of all clients to whom the order is applicable (not "et al"), and must include in item 8 of the proposed order any hearings "and other proceedings (including discovery matters)" that are set in the matter. The declaration contains information not re-stated in the proposed Order. The proposed Order must be brought to the hearing. =(302/MJM)
